figures.caption: Figure 4. Qualitative comparison of harmonization results. We show multi-view renderings of the inserted assets before and after harmonization. For each scene, the first row presents the original insertion results, and the second row shows the harmonized results. By predicting assets with canonical pose and real-world scale, our model enables fully automatic insertion and harmonization without manual intervention. Across multiple views, the harmonized assets demonstrate improved consistency with surrounding environments.
Table 6. Ablation study of sphere initialization and gradient filtering strategies.

Figure 16. Qualitative results of harmonization. The first row presents the pre-harmonization outputs, while the second row shows the harmonized results. Examples are shown across multiple poses of the same asset within the scene to illustrate consistency and robustness.

Table 2. Multi-view comparison on 3DRealCar and CFV datasets. To validate the effectiveness of multi-view input, we report metrics using different numbers of input viewpoints. We randomly select n views as input and evaluate the resulting models across all viewpoints.

Figure 9. We visualize the Gaussian points to illustrate geometric quality. Compared to TRELLIS [56], our method achieves high-fidelity geometric reconstruction using substantially fewer Gaussian points.
Figure 13. Qualitative results of LGM, TGS, DGS on CFV [2].
Figure 7. Qualitative generalization results of single-image reconstruction on autonomous driving datasets.
Figure 14. Qualitative results of TRELLIS and ours on CFV [2].
Figure 12. Qualitative results of TRELLIS and ours on 3DRealCar [8].
Figure 5. The two base modules of the backbone network.
Figure 15. Qualitative results of camera parameters prediction.

Table 1. Quantitative comparison on 3DRealCar and CFV datasets. For fair comparison, we follow a single-view inference paradigm and compute metrics across all views for both our method and the baselines. All baseline-generated models are manually aligned to canonical pose and normalized to standard scale, whereas our method requires no additional manual adjustment. Chamfer Distance (CD) is multiplied by 1× 103, and F-score is computed with a threshold of 0.01.
Figure 11. Qualitative results of LGM, TGS, DGS on 3DRealCar [8].
